description: Graceful Shutdown and Interrupt Handling - Add signal handling to runner.py for SIGINT/SIGTERM, save state on interrupt, kill subprocess on timeout, add atexit handler, support 'interrupted' status on --resume.
steps: Add signal handling infrastructure to runner.py: import signal and atexit at top. Create a module-level _shutdown_handler function that saves state immediately and sets status to 'interrupted'. Register it for SIGINT and SIGTERM inside main() when mode3 is active.
On TimeoutExpired in Mode 3: after catching subprocess.TimeoutExpired, call process.kill() to terminate the zombie process (currently it just logs the timeout but doesn't kill the subprocess).
Add atexit handler: register an atexit function inside main() that calls state_mgr.save_state() if the status is still 'running' (guards against crashes).
On --resume: in the resume handling section, detect if state status is 'interrupted' and handle it the same as resuming from any other non-complete state (reset status to 'running' to continue).
Add 'interrupted' as a valid status in state_manager.py if needed (check DEFAULT_STATE docs)

Add tests in tests/test_runner.py or a new tests/test_graceful_shutdown.py: test that SIGINT handler saves state with 'interrupted' status using mock signals, test that subprocess.kill() is called after TimeoutExpired, test that --resume from 'interrupted' status continues execution, test atexit handler saves state when status is 'running'
acceptance_criteria: SIGINT/SIGTERM during Mode 3 execution saves state with status='interrupted'
Subprocess is killed (not just logged) after TimeoutExpired
atexit handler saves state if status is still 'running' on exit
findings: All 831 tests pass with 98.85% coverage. Signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM), atexit handler, resume from interrupted, and timeout logging with subprocess context all implemented and tested.
